title: [PhysicalWidgetName]
text: [PhysicalWidgetName] This section, as defined in the web.ini file, defines a single physical UX web widget for Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components, including the name of the widget class that implements the client (browser) side of the widget, the JavaScript, Module, and CSS URLs that are required to run the widget class, some placeholder layout HTML used by the IDE and IDE property definitions. Syntax Explain this code [PhysicalWidgetName] widget_class = WidgetClass {javascript = JavaScriptURLs } {module = ModuleURLs } {css = CSSURLs } {ulayout = IDEPlaceholderLayout } {properties = IDEPropertyDefinitions } {collection_widget_class = CollectionWidgetClass } {occurrence_widget_class = OccurrenceWidgetClass } {component_widget_class = ComponentWidgetClass } {uxInterfaceVersion = UXInterfaceVersionNumber } {declarations = Declarations } {script = FieldProcScript } {collection_script = CollectionProcScript } {occurrence_script = OccurrenceProcScript } {component_script = ComponentProcScript } Copy See the following table for syntax description: Table 1. Physical widgets syntax description Syntax Description PhysicalWidgetName Specifies the physical widget name as used by the logical widget definition. PhysicalWidgetName is not case sensitive. For details, see web.ini . WidgetClass Specifies the name (case-sensitive) of the widget class as it is registered in the Uniface Class Registry. For details, see web.ini . JavaScriptURLs Specifies a semicolon-separated list of URLs (case-sensitive) to JavaScript URLs that are (explicitly or implicitly) used by the widget class, where the URL is relative to the virtual-root or is absolute. For details, see web.ini . ModuleURLs Specifies a semicolon-separated list of URLs (case-sensitive) to JavaScript URLs that are (explicitly or implicitly) used by the widget class and need to be loaded as a JavaScript module, where the URL is relative to the virtual-root or is absolute. For details, see web.ini . CSSURLs Specifies a semicolon-separated list of URLs (case-sensitive) to CSS URLs that are (explicitly or implicitly) used by the widget class, where the URL is relative to the virtual-root or is absolute. For details, see web.ini . IDEPlaceholderLayout Specifies a semicolon-separated list of attributes used by the IDE in the Define Layout worksheet when using "copy as HTML". If this definition is not defined, the IDE is not able to generate a sensible placeholder for your widget. This definition uses the following syntax "TAG= TagName ;HTMLATTRIBUTES= HtmlAttributes ;CHARACTERS= Characters " , where: Syntax Description TagName Specifies the HTML element (case-sensitive) that will be generated as placeholder for the widget. HtmlAttributes Specifies an exclamation-semicolon-separated ( !;) list of HTML attribute-value pairs (case-sensitive) that will be generated as part of the placeholder for the widget. The syntax is " attribute 1 = value 1 {!; attribute 2 = value 2 {....; attribute N = value N }}}" , where: attribute: Specifies any valid attribute for your widget value: Specifies the value of the attribute. Use keyword ubinding to instruct the IDE to generate the binding information for your entity, occurrence, or field; typically used in combination with the id attribute. Characters Specifies the inner text (case-sensitive) of the HTML element that will be used for the widget's placeholder. IDEPropertyDefinitions Specifies a list of property ids supported by your widget. This list is used by the Property Inspector in the IDE providing widget configuration assistance to any developer that uses your widget. The uproperties.ini file specifies the syntax of each property. This syntax drives the editing experience for that property in the Property Inspector. For more details, see uproperties.ini . This definition uses the following syntax " PropertyDefinition 1 {; PropertyDefinition 2 {...; PropertyDefinition N }} ", where PropertyDefinition specifies either the literal property-id or a property-group; property-groups are specified in the [propertygroups] section of the uproperties.ini file. CollectionWidgetClass Specifies the name (case-sensitive) of the collection widget class as it is registered in the Uniface Class Registry. For more information, see web.ini . OccurrenceWidgetClass Specifies the name (case-sensitive) of the occurrence widget class as it is registered in the Uniface Class Registry. For more information, see web.ini . ComponentWidgetClass Specifies the name (case-sensitive) of the component widget class as it is registered in the Uniface Class Registry. For more information, see web.ini . UXInterfaceVersionNumber Specifies the version of the UX interface used to implement the widget. Declarations Specifies the IncludeScript containing the declarations that can be used by ProcScript. FieldProcScript Specifies the IncludeScript that contains fragments of ProcScript or JavaScript for field-level UX widgets. CollectionProcScript Specifies the IncludeScript that contains fragments of ProcScript or JavaScript for collection-level UX widgets. OccurrenceProcScript Specifies the IncludeScript that contains fragments of ProcScript or JavaScript for the occurrence-level UX widgets. ComponentProcScript Specifies the IncludeScript that contains fragments of ProcScript or JavaScript for the component-level UX widgets. Note: The expression $LIBURL can be used inside a value of CSS or JavaScript to specify URLs relative to the location of the Uniface JavaScript library. The webdefinitions statement will replace it with the value of assignment setting $UNIFACE_RUNTIME_BASE_URL . Example 1: Explain this code [customFieldWidget] widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleFieldWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-field-widget.js ulayout = TAG=span;CHARACTERS=placeholder;HTMLATTRIBUTES=id=ubinding declarations = INCLIB:SIMPLE_DECLARATIONS script = INCLIB:SIMPLE_INCLUDESCRIPT uxInterfaceVersion = 2 Copy Example 2: Explain this code [customEntityWidget] occurrence_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleOccurrenceWidget collection_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleCollectionWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-occ-widget.js;$LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-coll-widget.js declarations = INCLIB:SIMPLE_DECLARATIONS collection_script = INCLIB:SIMPLE_COLL_INCLUDESCRIPT occurrence_script = INCLIB:SIMPLE_OCC_INCLUDESCRIPT uxInterfaceVersion = 2 Copy Example 3: Explain this code [customComponentWidget] component_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleComponentWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-comp-widget.js; declarations = INCLIB:SIMPLE_DECLARATIONS component_script = INCLIB:SIMPLE_COMP_INCLUDESCRIPT uxInterfaceVersion = 2 Copy Related Links web.ini The web.ini file defines the physical UX web widget definitions where every section is a single widget definition. Each widget definition includes the name of the widget class that implements the client (browser) side of the widget, the JavaScript, Module, and CSS files that are required to run the widget class, some placeholder layout HTML used by the IDE and IDE property definitions, IncludeScript which refers to the widget specific ProcScript or JavaScript fragments to be included and the Interface Version using which the widget is implemented. uproperties.ini The uproperties.ini file defines the syntax of properties as well as property groups for web widgets.
